insight: Uruguay's performance after hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup has been underwhelming, as they failed to gain momentum in both of their breaks, with a mean change of -0.100 minutes per game. When Uruguay was pressing before a break, they still struggled to maintain their momentum, with an average change of -0.100 minutes per game, and they were unable to keep their momentum going after the break. Overall, the hydration breaks have not provided Uruguay with the spark they needed, and instead, have seen their performance decline, regardless of whether they were pressing or not before the break.
